close_reason: Added empty BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN rejection with Unavailable error
description: src/lib.rs:125-129 calls std::env::var("BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN"). If the variable is set but is the empty string, var() returns Ok("") and the empty token gets stored in the backend. The error then surfaces only on the first get() call as a remote 4xx — by which time the user has built the backend, registered it, and possibly distributed it to long-running tasks. Fail fast: after env::var, check token.is_empty() and return SecretError::Unavailable with a message distinguishing 'not set' from 'set but empty'. Same treatment is also worth considering for tokens that obviously do not match the bitwarden access-token format ('0.<uuid>.<secret>:<key>'), though that's a separate finding if you want to track it.
title: from_uri does not reject empty BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN
